summary: Partially decompiled Sheik's chain item code by replacing several m2c stubs in `itseakchain.c`, naming the spawn entrypoint as `itSeakChain_Spawn`, tightening related prototypes, and clarifying the chain item variable field that stores the owning fighter GObj. The PR also added a helper shell function for broad source-symbol replacement. Evidence for reviewer intent is limited because there were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Renamed symbol `it_802BB290` to `itSeakChain_Spawn` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, the Sheik side-special caller, item headers, and dox declarations.

Implemented `itSeakChain_Spawn` in `src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c`; it builds a `SpawnItem` for `It_Kind_Seak_Chain`, initializes parent fields, zeroes chain-related item-var storage using `M2C_FIELD`, calls `it_80272A3C`, initializes against Sheik's `FtPart_L3rdNa` joint via `it_802BAF2C`, and attaches the item to the fighter with `Item_8026AB54`.
Renamed `itSeakChain_ItemVars.x8` to `parent_gobj`, then updated chain cleanup, hitbox update, collision, item event, and accessory paths to use the clearer field name.

Added C implementations for previously stubbed chain routines including `it_802BB938`, `it_802BBD64`, and `it_802BBED0`, covering chain segment collision state, position propagation, and return modes used by accessory callbacks.

Adjusted `fn_802BB44C` and `fn_802BB574` to pass the matrix argument into `it_802BBD64` and `it_802BBED0` instead of the `itSeakChain_Attrs*`, and updated the corresponding header prototypes to accept `Mtx`.
Updated `it_802BAF2C` prototype from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to `int it_802BAF2C(Item* ip, HSD_JObj* jobj)`.
Added includes needed by the new code, including fighter inlines/forward declarations, `it_2725`, `lb/lbaudio_ax`, and `mp/mpcoll`.
Added `_m2c_stack_it_802BB290` stack layout with a `SpawnItem` member and `STATIC_ASSERT`, plus a new zero vector symbol `it_803B8680` in `itseakchain.static.h`.
Added `melee_sd` to `tools/functions.sh`, a helper wrapping `sd` over source, docs, symbols, splits, ldscripts, and configure files.
smart_moves: The spawn function was named semantically as `itSeakChain_Spawn` once the call site in `ftSk_SpecialS_CheckInitChain` made its role clear.
Renaming the item-var field from `x8` to `parent_gobj` made multiple later call sites self-documenting, especially cleanup and collision code that interact with the owning fighter.
The PR improved prototypes at the same time as matching code, reducing reliance on `UNK_PARAMS` and mismatched argument types.
Using `M2C_FIELD` preserved partially understood layout accesses for the chain segment vector/array area instead of inventing a full struct layout prematurely.
The inline comment on `itSeakChain_Spawn` explicitly marks the zeroed `0xDF0`-range storage as probably a Vec3 array, documenting uncertainty without overcommitting.
Adding a stack-layout static assert for the spawned item function records known stack evidence and supports future matching work.
The `melee_sd` helper institutionalizes broad symbol rename workflow across source, docs, config, and symbols files.
decomp_lessons: When a formerly numeric item function has a single meaningful fighter call site, use the call site semantics to rename both the symbol file and all declarations consistently.
Do not wait for a perfect struct before matching: use `M2C_FIELD` for unknown offsets and annotate likely structure patterns, such as suspected Vec3 arrays.
Header cleanup is part of matching work; replacing `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` with concrete types prevents later call-site drift.
If a function actually consumes matrix elements such as `mtx[0][1]` and `mtx[1][2]`, update the prototype to carry `Mtx` rather than forcing it through an unrelated attrs struct.
Field names should capture ownership semantics. `parent_gobj` communicates why fighter collision and hitbox helpers are called through this stored GObj.
For chain or hookshot-like item systems, the item code may coordinate item links, fighter hitboxes, collision data, audio, and bone attachment in one matching unit.
naming_conventions: `itSeakChain_Spawn` uses the project item naming pattern `it<Character><Item>_<Verb>` for a now-understood spawn routine.
`parent_gobj` replaces an offset-style field name when the stored GObj is known to be the owning fighter.
The existing file keeps many unresolved functions with address-style names such as `it_802BB938`, `it_802BBD64`, and `it_802BBED0` when semantics are not yet strong enough for a descriptive name.
The Sheik fighter code uses `ftSk_SpecialS_CheckInitChain` and stores the spawned chain in `fp->fv.sk.x8` and `fp->x1984_heldItemSpec`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved matching-sensitive control flow in `it_802BBD64` and `it_802BBED0` using explicit labels and `goto` structure.
Used `PAD_STACK` and a dedicated `_m2c_stack_it_802BB290` static stack struct to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Kept unresolved memory layout accesses as explicit offset writes with `M2C_FIELD(ip, f32*, 0xDF0..0xE4C)` while matching initialization behavior.
Converted parenthesized arithmetic like `(origin.x * scale) + offset->x` to `origin.x * scale + offset->x`, likely to match compiler expression output.
Matched bitfield behavior in `it_802BB938` using direct byte access through `M2C_FIELD(link, u8*, 0x2C)` alongside named bitfields such as `x2C_b0`, `x2C_b1`, and `x2C_b2`.
Used matrix element indexing directly for chain length/gravity-like parameters, for example `mtx[0][1]` and `mtx[1][2]`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
There was one issue comment counted, but the provided excerpt contains no body text, so no specific human feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace `M2C_FIELD` accesses around offsets `0xDF0` to `0xE4C` in `itSeakChain_ItemVars` with a real Vec3 array or chain segment storage.
Search for later renames of `it_802BB938`, `it_802BBD64`, and `it_802BBED0` once their chain physics/collision roles are better understood.
Check whether `it_802BAF2C` was later named and whether its prototype remained `int it_802BAF2C(Item* ip, HSD_JObj* jobj)`.
Look for future use of `melee_sd` or similar symbol replacement tooling in mass rename PRs.
Compare this chain implementation with Link hookshot item code to identify reusable matching patterns for linked item segments.
